# Deep-Static Curated Fixtures

These fixtures cover local source archives, isolated PGP verification paths,
and text-only deep-static source inspection. The archives and signatures are
generated by `tests/helpers/archive_fixtures.py` inside pytest temp
directories, so the fixture tree stays text-only and safe to inspect.

Each scenario contains a `PKGBUILD` template and an `expected.json` manifest.
The test runner replaces checksum and fingerprint placeholders after creating
the local source archive. No fixture runs package code, calls makepkg, contacts
the network, installs packages, requires root, or uses the user's GPG keyring.

Archive scenarios:

- `archive_path_traversal`: tar entry uses `../evil.txt`.
- `archive_absolute_path`: tar entry uses an absolute path.
- `archive_symlink_escape`: tar symlink points outside the extraction root.
- `archive_hardlink_escape`: tar hardlink points outside the extraction root.
- `archive_too_many_files`: archive exceeds a small test file-count limit.
- `archive_oversized`: archive exceeds a small test decompressed-size limit.
- `archive_nested_depth`: nested archive name exceeds a small test depth limit.

PGP scenarios:

- `pgp_valid_signature`: mocked isolated GPG status verifies the declared full fingerprint.
- `pgp_invalid_signature`: mocked isolated GPG status reports an invalid signature.
- `pgp_signer_mismatch`: mocked isolated GPG status reports a signer different from `validpgpkeys`.
- `pgp_key_unavailable`: no test key is supplied, so verification falls back to manual review.
- `pgp_weak_validpgpkeys`: `validpgpkeys` uses a short key ID and must not auto-fetch.

Source-content scenarios:

- `source_setup_py`: harmless `setup.py` text contains a subprocess indicator.
- `source_package_json`: harmless `package.json` text contains a postinstall script.
- `source_token_reference`: harmless source text references fake token variable names.
- `source_vendored_deps`: source tree contains a vendored dependency directory.
- `source_minified_file`: source tree contains a generated-looking minified file.
- `deep_static_systemd_unit_file`: source tree contains a harmless `.service` file.
- `deep_static_systemd_auto_enable`: source text contains a defanged `systemctl enable`.
- `deep_static_systemd_user_persistence`: source text references a fake user service path.
- `systemd_docs_no_warning`: comment-only systemd documentation does not raise a systemd finding.
- `cron_docs_no_warning`: comment-only cron documentation does not raise a cron finding.
